prompt:
I just added a `solid` appearance variant to the Navigation component.
Did you document this variant in the playground? You almost never do.
Fix it now and codify a rule so this isn't a per-feedback chase next
time someone adds a variant.
response:
I now have a complete picture. Here is the full diagnosis and fix: --- ## Diagnosis **The playground page was already partially written** — `codeSolid` string and a `` block exist in `playground/src/app/components/navigation/page.tsx`. But the `Navigation.module.scss` **has no `.solid` class**. The TSX applies `styles.solid` when `appearance === "solid"`, but that class was never added to the SCSS. The playground renders `` and it looks identical to `frosted` because the modifier rule is absent — the variant is visually undocumented in the most important way: it doesn't actually show the different appearance. **Root causes:** | # | Anti-pattern | Evidence | |---|---|---| | **EAP-082** | *Adding a Variant/Prop to a DS Component Without Updating the Playground Page* | `solid` variant added to `Navigation.tsx` and `NavigationAppearance` type, but the SCSS modifier class was never created and the playground preview is a no-op | | **EAP-008** | *Documenting Recurring Fixes in Docs Instead of Promoting to Rules* | This feedback mirrors ENG-106 exactly — a variant shipped without playground documentation — yet the rule file (`.cursor/rules/playground-components.md`) still has no enforcement clause that fires at component-authoring time | --- ## Fix — Part 1: The Missing SCSS `.solid` Class **File:** `src/components/ui/Navigation/Navigation.module.scss` Add the `.solid` modifier block immediately after `.nav { … }`: ```scss // appearance="solid" — fully opaque surface, no backdrop blur. // Use when the nav should sit as a clean boundary rather than float over content. .solid { background-color: var(--portfolio-surface-terra-05); backdrop-filter: none; -webkit-backdrop-filter: none; &::after { // Disable the frosted-glass bleed shadow that the default variant uses. display: none; } } ``` > **Why `--portfolio-surface-terra-05`?** The base `.nav` already uses `--portfolio-surface-terra-subtle` (the translucent version). `terra-05` is the fully opaque equivalent and is how `solid` surfaces are defined elsewhere in the system. If the token name differs in your current token set, substitute the correct fully-opaque terra token — do not hardcode a hex value (guardrail: `.cursor/rules/playground-components.md` §Non-Negotiable #2). --- ## Fix — Part 2: The Playground Page Is Correct, But Verify the Preview Actually Shows a Difference **File:** `playground/src/app/components/navigation/page.tsx` The page already has the right structure — two `` blocks, a props table row, and a notes section. **No content changes needed.** Once the SCSS `.solid` class exists, the `appearance="solid"` render will visually differ from `frosted` and the documentation will be accurate. Double-check the preview wrapper gives the contrast you need to see the difference (a dark or content-colored background behind the solid nav): ```tsx // Wrap the solid preview in a slightly darker surface so the // opaque background registers visually against the playground shell.
``` > Note: the `style={{ background }}` is a token reference, not a hex value — this is acceptable per the playground guardrails. If you'd prefer to avoid inline style entirely, add a utility class to `playground/src/app/globals.css` and use that instead. --- ## Fix — Part 3: Codify the Rule So This Doesn't Require Per-Feedback Chase This is the structural fix. The current rule file only covers the Intent Gate (don't edit playground to fix visual bugs) and the flush-restart protocol. It says nothing about the **obligation to update the playground when a variant is added to the source component.** **File:** `.cursor/rules/playground-components.md` Add this section **before** the existing "Non-Negotiable" block: ```markdown ## Variant/Prop Completeness (BLOCKING — runs when editing any `src/components/ui/` file) When you add, remove, or rename **any of the following** in a DS component: - A prop (new name or new union member, e.g., `appearance = "solid"`) - A visual state (e.g., `disabled`, `loading`, `active`) - A slot or sub-component (e.g., `NavigationBadge`) You **MUST** do all four of the following before closing the task — in order: 1. **Add the SCSS modifier class** that makes the variant visually distinct. A prop that applies `styles.solid` when the class doesn't exist in the SCSS is not a variant — it is dead code. The SCSS class is the implementation; the prop is the API. Both must ship together. 2. **Add a `` block** to `playground/src/app/components//page.tsx` that renders the component with the new prop value. One block per variant. 3. **Add a row to the ``** for every new prop (or update the `type` string for a new union member on an existing prop). 4. **Run the flush-restart protocol** (see §Flush-and-Restart Gate) and verify the new preview block appears in the HTTP response. **Verification question (ask yourself before marking done):** > "If I open the playground `/components/` page right now, can a reader see > every documented variant rendered side-by-side?" If the answer is no, you are not done. See EAP-082. > This rule closes the gap that produced ENG-106 (variant shipped, playground not > updated) and prevents EAP-008 (pattern re-documented in feedback logs rather > than promoted to an enforced rule). ``` --- ## After the SCSS fix — mandatory flush-and-restart Per `.cursor/rules/playground-components.md` §Flush-and-Restart Gate (and AGENTS.md guardrail #11), run: ```bash # 1. Kill playground server lsof -ti :4001 | xargs kill -9 # 2. Clear Turbopack cache rm -rf playground/.next # 3. Restart npm run playground & # 4. Wait for 200, then verify both variants are in the HTML curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:4001/components/navigation curl -s http://localhost:4001/components/navigation | grep -c 'appearance="solid"' # Expected: 2 (one in the preview, one in the code block) ``` --- ## Principle **EAP-082** exists precisely because a prop and its SCSS implementation, its playground preview, and its props table row are three separate files that must ship as one atomic unit — the human eye sees them as one feature, but they live in `src/`, `src/styles/`, and `playground/` respectively. The new rule in `.cursor/rules/playground-components.md` makes the checklist explicit and blocking at authoring time, not reactive at feedback time. This closes the **EAP-008** loop: the pattern was documented in the feedback log (ENG-106) but never promoted to an enforced rule that would fire during the next component edit. Now it is.
